title: BCM Stellar Wave Registry -- Scale-Invariance Test Dataset v7
author: Stephen Justin Burdick Sr. -- Emerald Entities LLC
description: Real published stellar parameters for BCM tachocline substrate wave solver. All values from peer-reviewed sources or documented stellar structure scaling. No per-star tuning. Same wave equation as galactic and planetary solvers. rho_tachocline added v7. B_tachocline updated from surface proxies to internal estimates where available.
note: m_observed = azimuthal mode number inferred from dominant observable pattern. 0 = unknown/anomalous. rho_tachocline_kgm3 = plasma density at tachocline interface.
calibration_method: Solar reference: rho=200 kg/m3 at 0.713 R_sun (Christensen-Dalsgaard 1996). Other stars scaled from mean density * depth fraction. B_tachocline from helioseismology (Sun) or dynamo model estimates.

Sun.observable: 4-sector solar wind pattern; Hale 22-year magnetic cycle (m=2 magnetic, m=4 wind)
Sun.notes: Control star. B_tachocline calibrated to 5.6T from helioseismology range 1-10T (2026-04-02).

citations.sigma: Spitzer 1962 -- plasma conductivity at tachocline conditions
citations.v_conv: Miesch et al. 2008, ApJ 673 -- helioseismic inversions

Tabby.observable: Aperiodic flux dips 0.1-22% (Boyajian 2016). No clean periodic signal.
Tabby.notes: KIC 8462852. F-type shallow CZ. B_tachocline estimated from dynamo scaling. rho from stellar structure scaling (low density, large R).

Proxima.observable: Single active longitude. Superflares. 83.5d rotation period.
Proxima.notes: Fully convective -- no tachocline. m=1 dipole mode from tachocline gate. rho estimated for fully convective interior.

citations.fully_convective: Chabrier & Baraffe 1997 -- M < 0.35 Msun fully convective

EV_Lac.observable: Two active longitude flare clustering. Rapid rotator.
EV_Lac.notes: Near fully-convective boundary. B_tachocline calibrated 2026-04-02. rho estimated for deep CZ.

citations.two_active_long: Bidin et al. 2006 -- active longitude clustering in M flare stars

HR_1099.observable: Persistent two active longitude structure. RS CVn binary (K1IV + G5V).
HR_1099.notes: RS CVn binary V711 Tau. B_tachocline calibrated 2026-04-02. Tidal forcing = bar-channeling analog. Standard Alfven Hamiltonian predicts m=12 -- needs tidal term (v7 target).

Epsilon_Eri.observable: Active young solar analog. 3-sector wind structure from ZDI mapping.
Epsilon_Eri.notes: Young active K dwarf. B_tachocline estimated from dynamo scaling. rho from stellar structure scaling.

citations.B_mapping: Jeffers et al. 2014, A&A 569 -- ZDI magnetic maps

Alpha_Cen_A.observable: Solar twin. Magnetic activity comparable to Sun. Deep Maunder-like minimum since 2005.
Alpha_Cen_A.notes: Solar analog calibration. B_tachocline = solar value. rho from stellar structure scaling (larger R = lower density at tachocline). Binary with Alpha Cen B -- substrate bridge target.

citations.activity: Ayres 2014, AJ 147 -- X-ray cycles Alpha Cen AB

Alpha_Cen_B.observable: K-dwarf secondary. Long rotation period. Known activity cycle.
Alpha_Cen_B.notes: Binary companion to Alpha Cen A. B_tachocline from K-dwarf dynamo scaling. rho from stellar structure scaling. Predicted m=5 with current values -- K-dwarf calibration boundary. Substrate bridge target with Alpha Cen A.

61_Cyg_A.observable: K-dwarf with solar-like magnetic cycle. High proper motion.
61_Cyg_A.notes: B_tachocline from K-dwarf dynamo scaling. rho from stellar structure scaling (deep CZ = high density). Predicted m=6 -- calibration boundary for late K dwarfs.

AU_Mic.observable: Young flare star. 4.84d rotation. Debris disk detected. Two active longitudes.
AU_Mic.notes: Rapid rotator near fully convective boundary. Standard Alfven Hamiltonian predicts m=12 -- same boundary problem as HR_1099. Possible Class II tidal/rotational override needed.

Vega.observable: A-star rapid rotator. Weak magnetic field. Nearly pole-on orientation.
Vega.notes: Very thin convective envelope. Rapid rotation drives extreme ΩR_tach. Predicted high-m mode. BCM prediction target -- no confirmed m_observed.

citations.B_field: Lignieres et al. 2009, A&A 500 -- sub-gauss detection

Spica_A.observable: Beta Cephei variable. Primary component of Spica binary (P_orb=4.014d, e=0.13).
Spica_A.notes: Massive B-type star. No traditional tachocline -- substrate pump driven by iron opacity Z-layer instability. Standard Alfven Hamiltonian does not apply (predicts m=24+). m_observed=0 pending BCM-specific calibration. Beta Cephei pulsation modes are p/g-modes, not necessarily BCM substrate modes. Binary substrate bridge target with Spica B.

citations.pulsation: Shobbrook et al. 1969 -- Beta Cephei classification

Spica_B.observable: Secondary in eccentric binary. Rotationally broadened (v sin i = 58 km/s).
Spica_B.notes: Companion to Spica A. No traditional tachocline. Standard Alfven Hamiltonian does not apply. m_observed=0 pending calibration. Eccentric orbit (e=0.13) creates time-variable substrate coupling -- analog to variable SMBH torque at galactic scale.
